case_metadata.case_name: Diamond v. St. Anthony Community Hosp.

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The plaintiff presented to the emergency department of the defendant St. Anthony Community Hospital, Warwick, New York (hereinafter the hospital), after a boating accident and was seen by the defendant Rebeca Caban, an obstetrician/gynecologist, who made an initial diagnosis of a peritoneal laceration.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action, inter alia, to
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiff presented to ED after a boating accident; diagnosed with peritoneal laceration.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Caban took the plaintiff into surgery, during which Caban diagnosed the plaintiff with a deep gluteal laceration, which Caban cleaned and repaired.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action, inter alia, to
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiff underwent surgery for a deep gluteal laceration.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Approximately one week later, the plaintiff presented to a different hospital where she was diagnosed with a surgical wound infection that required further surgery for drainage and debridement of the wound and broad spectrum antibiotics.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action, inter alia, to
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiff developed a surgical wound infection requiring further surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Ackerman's affirmation raised triable issues of fact, as she contradicted Caban's proffered expert opinion that it was proper for Caban to close the plaintiff's wound completely and to not insert a drain.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action, inter alia, to
evidence_index.summary: Expert Ackerman contradicted Caban's decision to close the wound completely.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Stein opined, in contrast, that post-discharge antibiotics were warranted based upon the origin and nature of the wound, the prolonged exposure to nonsterile conditions prior to surgery, and lab results showing elevated neutrophils, an early warning sign of infection.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action, inter alia, to
evidence_index.summary: Expert Stein opined that post-discharge antibiotics were warranted.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Accordingly, the Supreme Court should have denied those branches of the defendants' separate motions which were for summary judgment dismissing the cause of action alleging medical malpractice insofar as asserted against each of them.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action, inter alia, to
evidence_index.summary: Court reversed summary judgment dismissing the malpractice claim.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

verdict_detected.key_phrase: Accordingly, the Supreme Court should have denied those branches of the defendants' separate motions which were for summary judgment dismissing the cause of action alleging medical malpractice insofar as asserted against each of them.

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient presented to the emergency department after a boating accident.

focused_exam.abdomen: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt consideration of infection risk

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in surgery for a deep gluteal laceration
decision_points.prompt: How should the surgical wound be managed?

options.description: Close the wound completely without a drain
options.clinical_reasoning: Defendant believed complete closure was appropriate

options.description: Leave the wound partially open and insert a drain
options.clinical_reasoning: Expert opinion suggested this to prevent infection

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed it was proper to close completely

expected_action_court.description: Wound should have been left partially open with a drain
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated this was necessary

explanation.what_should_have_happened: Wound management should have included a drain

options.clinical_reasoning: Expert opinion indicated antibiotics were warranted

explanation.what_should_have_happened: Antibiotics should have been prescribed post-discharge

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED after a boating accident

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed a surgical wound infection requiring further surgery

key_findings: Breach of duty in wound management and failure to prescribe antibiotics
Causation established - infection resulted from improper wound management

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to manage wound properly and prescribe antibiotics
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper wound management and antibiotics could have prevented infection

factual_timeline.event: Patient presents to ED after boating accident

allegations.allegation: Failure to manage wound properly and prescribe antibiotics
